findings.locus: Decision — 'This record fixes the function, its constant treatment, its totality contract, its composition boundary, and its calibration governance.'
findings.claim: The title, summary (file Description), and status frame DDR-004 as fixing the inherited-confidence derivation, but §4 and §6 commit determinations that are DDR-002 schema amendments — per-node-label declared-basis carriers on PlaneDefinition (§2.6), core-plane basis annotations (§2.2–2.5), the DeploymentEnvironment basis-2 annotation, and the Evidence.observed_at semantic — which the record itself states are 'DDR-002 amendments (Pre-Acceptance Conditions)' tracked on RBT-54. These are decisions the record depends on and partly pre-decides (e.g. 'DeploymentEnvironment = basis 2, observed_at-absence ratified correct-by-design') while the title claims only a derivation-function ruling; whether the record decides the DDR-002 amendment content or merely entails it is not cleanly separated in the claim surface.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §4 (per-class dispositions + 'These are DDR-002 amendments (Pre-Acceptance Conditions)') and §6 (Evidence.observed_at semantic) vs. DDR-004 Decision/title claim scope

findings.authority_locus: deliberation-record-ddr-004, Authoring notes ('DDR-002 mechanization not authored here') + Item 4/6 rulings, and author-decision-record SKILL, DDR routing ('Keep the record's body to the contract'; DDR shape — Pre-Acceptance/Migration Conditions with tracking pointers)

findings.locus: Reconciliation with the SDD-001 interim — 'the SDD needs no content change — the pointer resolution is a change-log-carried amendment on the SDD side'
findings.claim: DDR-004 asserts SDD-001 needs no content change because §3.4.3/§9 route by subject-name. But SDD-001 §3.4.3 as fetched states 'Extension-plane nodes take branch (i) where they carry a node confidence, else — where they carry observed_at — Environment-class treatment (contested)' and rejects only the confidence-less, timestamp-less Extension citation via CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE. DDR-004 §4 now reclassifies totality as a per-class declared-basis property (four bases, CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE demoted to defense-in-depth unreachable for well-formed planes). The interim SDD text describes CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE as the primary reachable control for a known Extension case; DDR-004 makes it a backstop only. This is a substantive change in what the SDD's implemented behavior describes, not merely a pointer resolution — the claim that no content change is needed is not supported by the cited SDD substrate.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §4 + Reconciliation section vs. SDD-001 v1.0.0 §3.4.3 (Extension-branch totality / CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE as primary reachable case)

findings.locus: §4 Per-class declared-basis totality — 'A citation that reaches no branch is rejected, typed CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE, fail-closed' vs. §1 Totality
findings.claim: The record claims totality is 'total by construction' because 'every citable node-class carries a declared basis (§4),' and simultaneously that CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE is 'unreachable for any well-formed plane.' But the deliberation-grounded honest floor stated in §4 ('whether reasoners cite DeploymentEnvironment, RateCard, or CostFactor is unverified') and the n=0 traffic floor mean the totality property is asserted for the current declared planes only. The record does not declare a consequence/side-effect: a newly-registered Extension plane that declares a citable node with no basis is said to 'fail registration' (§4), but the enforcement of that fail-registration is routed to a DDR-002 amendment not yet made (RBT-54 Touch 3). Until that carrier exists, 'total by construction' is a claimed property whose enforcing mechanism is a declared dependency that does not yet exist — the record asserts the totality guarantee while its enforcement artifact is unbuilt.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §1 ('total by construction') + §4 ('fails registration' / 'These are DDR-002 amendments (Pre-Acceptance Conditions)') vs. §Pre-Acceptance condition 1 (carriers tracked RBT-54 Touch 3, not yet authored)

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL (ddr-template — 'Pre-Acceptance / Migration Conditions' section: conditions enumerated as discrete buildable prerequisites with tracking pointers; Status lifecycle 'ACCEPTED-WITH-CONDITIONS when the design is sound but blocked on prerequisites') + DDR-004 §Pre-Acceptance condition 1 (RBT-54 Touch 3 carrier)

findings.locus: Rationale + Pre-Acceptance / Reconciliation — instantiated run/ticket identifiers in normative body
findings.claim: The record's Rationale, Pre-Acceptance Conditions, and Reconciliation sections carry instantiated process identifiers (run-009 R6, runs 010/011/012, RBT-53, RBT-54 Touch 3, union disposition Item 1) threaded through normative prose rather than confined to Change Log / Cross-References. The record's own deliberation-record notes flag this as a docs-structure contract-purity miss to be cleared before push; from claim-fidelity altitude the concern is that process narrative in the body dilutes what the record decides versus how it came to be decided.

cited_authority.ref: author-decision-record SKILL, Authoring discipline — 'Keep the record's body to the contract. Process narrative, ticket IDs, and review history belong in the Change Log, Cross-References, or your tracker — not threaded through the body.'

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL, Authoring discipline — 'Keep the record's body to the contract. Process narrative, ticket IDs, and review history belong in the Change Log, Cross-References, or your tracker — not threaded through the body.'

findings.locus: §2 Constant treatment — the 1.0 invariant-ground vs. contested-tunable split
findings.claim: I attacked the claim that fixing 1.0 as invariant-ground (not a §4.6 tunable) over-decides beyond what this record's stated scope permits — testing whether it smuggles a determination DDR-002 §4 already owns or contradicts. The record cleanly declares 1.0 as 'a determination this record makes on canonical warrant, not a value DDR-002 carries,' and names the canonical support (DDR-002 §4 fixes inheritance by authority class and the ordering, not the literal 1.0). The decision content matches the claimed scope: it decides the constant treatment it says it decides, and the dependency on DDR-002 §4 is named. The claim-fidelity attack failed — the scope boundary and its cited authority hold.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §2 vs. DDR-002 v1.2.0 §4 (canonical confidence definition — inheritance by authority class, ordering, #24 ceiling)

findings.locus: §5 Composition boundary — branch (i) inherits node authority only; per-target edge certainty routed to the ReasoningProgress rollup
findings.claim: I attacked §5 as a potential quiet retraction — that declaring the OBSERVED_IN per-target certainty 'not this derivation's' responsibility might silently drop a consequence DDR-002 §4/§3 requires the derivation to carry. The record declares the side effect explicitly ('The only residue is indirection'), names the downstream owner (the ReasoningProgress rollup, routed to the solutioning-agent SDD per SDD-001 §2.2), and states the boundary is 'documented, not amended.' The consequence is surfaced rather than smuggled, and the composition-owner dependency is named. The attack — that a boundary was drawn to shed a declared obligation without declaring it — did not land.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §5 vs. DDR-002 v1.2.0 §4 (#24 ceiling / rollup routing) + §3 (edge vs. node confidence surfaces) + SDD-001 §2.2 (rollup routed to solutioning-agent SDD)

findings.locus: §6 Reconciliation — Δt reconstructibility / Evidence.observed_at semantic
findings.claim: The record's calibration reconstructibility rests on the claim that Evidence.observed_at 'is' the capture instant, but DDR-002 §4 defines Evidence.observed_at within the Evidence node schema without stating it is the capture instant, and §3 of this record independently defines Δt's 'from' point as the cited node's observed_at (source freshness), not Evidence's own observed_at. The record itself flags this as unconfirmed ('Evidence.observed_at's intended meaning must be confirmed against DDR-002 before adoption'), so the durable-recoverability property the calibration path (§6) depends on is asserted against an authority the record concedes it has not verified.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 v1.2.0 §4 (:Reasoning:Evidence schema — observed_at property, no capture-instant definition)

findings.locus: §4 Per-class declared-basis totality — DeploymentEnvironment basis-2 assignment
findings.claim: §4 assigns Environment DeploymentEnvironment basis 2 with 'the Environment base 0.9, contested per §2, decay term dropped.' But §2 defines base=0.9 as the Environment aging constant paired with τ, calibrated 'to the baseline reliability of fresh Environment facts' decayed over time (§6). Reusing 0.9 as a flat, non-decaying base for a structurally-stable node applies a constant whose entire calibration rationale (§6: base fit to fresh-fact reliability against outcomes, τ fit to staleness timescale) presupposes the decay term the assignment drops — the value's substantiation in §2/§6 does not cover a non-aging use, leaving the DeploymentEnvironment base uncalibrated by any stated basis.

cited_authority.ref: internal inconsistency between §4 DeploymentEnvironment basis-2 use of 0.9 and §2/§6 calibration rationale for base=0.9 (aging-only)

findings.locus: Cross-References / normative body — instantiated identifiers in contract sections
findings.claim: Normative body sections (Rationale, Reconciliation with the SDD-001 interim, Pre-Acceptance/Migration Conditions) carry instantiated run/ticket identifiers (run-009 R6, runs 010/011/012, RBT-53, RBT-54 Touch 3, union disposition Item 1) rather than confining them to Change Log / Cross-References, deviating from the authoring-discipline convention that process narrative, ticket IDs, and review history belong in the Change Log, Cross-References, or tracker — not threaded through the body.

cited_authority.ref: author-decision-record-SKILL — Authoring discipline: 'Keep the record's body to the contract. Process narrative, ticket IDs, and review history belong in the Change Log, Cross-References, or your tracker — not threaded through the body.'

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record-SKILL — Authoring discipline: 'Keep the record's body to the contract. Process narrative, ticket IDs, and review history belong in the Change Log, Cross-References, or your tracker — not threaded through the body.'

findings.locus: §4 / Pre-Acceptance Conditions — DDR-002 amendments as pre-mechanization dependency
findings.claim: The record fixes per-class declared-basis totality (§4) and states the declared basis 'is carried in the schema … These are DDR-002 amendments (Pre-Acceptance Conditions),' yet the totality property the derivation's soundness rests on ('For every citable node-class exactly one branch yields a value, because every citable class carries a declared basis (§4)', §1) is unenforceable until those DDR-002 amendments land. The record claims totality is 'total by construction' and 'established at each citable node-class's definition boundary,' but the carriers that would make it so are un-ratified forward dependencies on RBT-54 — the claimed by-construction guarantee is not substantiated in the ratified schema as it stands.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §1 'total by construction' + §4 'established at each citable node-class's definition boundary' vs. DDR-004 Pre-Acceptance Condition 1 (declared-basis carriers are un-ratified DDR-002 amendments on RBT-54 Touch 3)

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL (ddr-template §Pre-Acceptance/Migration Conditions + Status-and-Version lifecycle) + DDR-004 deliberation record Item 4 / Authoring notes (RBT-54 Touch 3 routing) + ddr-template §Status (ACCEPTED-WITH-CONDITIONS for designs blocked on prerequisites)

findings.locus: References header — DDR-002 §2.2/§2.6 vs §2.2–2.5 citation
findings.claim: The References field and Cross-References cite 'DDR-002 §2.2/§2.6 plane schemas' and '§2.2 / §2.6 (Environment and Extension plane schemas),' but §4's declared-basis carriers span §2.2–§2.5 (core planes) per Pre-Acceptance Condition 1 ('core-plane bases are annotated in §2.2–2.5'). The header citation understates the loci the record actually amends, so a reader resolving cross-references from the header alone will not find the Catalog/Standards/Governance/Operational plane sections the ruling touches.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 References/Cross-References (§2.2/§2.6) vs. DDR-004 Pre-Acceptance Condition 1 (§2.2–2.5 core-plane annotations)

findings.authority_locus: DDR-004 Pre-Acceptance Condition 1 ('core-plane bases are annotated in §2.2–§2.5') — the record's own internally-ratified statement of the loci §4's declared-basis carriers span

findings.locus: §5 Composition boundary — branch (i) × #24 ceiling
findings.claim: Checked the branch-(i)-inherits-node-authority-only boundary against DDR-002 §4's #24 ceiling (conclusion bounded at max of supporting Evidence.confidence, weight below the ceiling only). Attacked it as a silent loss of the OBSERVED_IN per-target edge certainty: the record correctly locates that certainty on the edge (DDR-002 §3, node vs edge confidence split), routes its composition to the ReasoningProgress rollup (SDD-001 §2.2/§3.3.3 returns the two uncomposed), and the weakest-link ceiling direction (foreclose upward, permit downward via weight) is consistent with DDR-002 §4's stated bound. The boundary holds without deviation from the cited canon.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 v1.2.0 §4 (#24 ceiling = max supporting Evidence.confidence; weight below ceiling) + §3 (node/edge confidence split)

findings.locus: §1 / §4 Totality — derive-or-reject fail-closed against CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE
findings.claim: Checked the total-by-construction / derive-or-reject invariant against SDD-001 §3.4.3 (the implemented derivation) and §3.2 (CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE typed, fail-closed). Attacked it for a defaulting gap — a citation reaching no branch. The record's four-basis enumeration plus the fail-closed CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE backstop matches SDD-001's implemented behavior exactly (branch (i) native, branch (ii) class base × freshness, reject typed otherwise), and the demotion of CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE to defense-in-depth is consistent with the per-class basis declaration closing the reachable cases named in SDD-001 §3.4.3. No default-a-value path exists in the specified behavior.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 v1.0.0 §3.4.3 (capture-evidence derivation, total by construction) + §3.2 (CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE typed fail-closed)

findings.locus: §4 Per-class declared-basis totality + Pre-Acceptance Condition 1
findings.claim: The ruling's totality-by-construction property is load-bearing (branch (ii) totality, the CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE demotion to defense-in-depth) yet depends entirely on DDR-002 amendments that do not yet exist — the PlaneDefinition per-node-label basis declaration and the core-plane basis annotations are routed to RBT-54 Touch 3, downstream and unauthored. The record commits a total-by-construction contract against a schema that cannot yet carry the declared basis. This is a timing posture: the ruling's central invariant is stated as achieved when its enforcement carrier is a forthcoming, unratified amendment on another ticket.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §4 'These are DDR-002 amendments (Pre-Acceptance Conditions)' + Pre-Acceptance Condition 1 (RBT-54 Touch 3) vs. DDR-002 v1.2.0 §2.6 PlaneDefinition schema (no derivation-basis declaration field exists)

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record SKILL (DDR route) — Pre-Acceptance / Migration Conditions section: 'If the ruling is conditional — sound design, blocked on prerequisites — enumerate each condition as a discrete, buildable prerequisite with a tracking pointer,' plus the Status lifecycle 'ACCEPTED-WITH-CONDITIONS when the design is sound but blocked on prerequisites'; ddr-template Pre-Acceptance/Migration Conditions

findings.locus: §6 Reconciliation / §3 Reconstructibility + Pre-Acceptance Condition 2
findings.claim: The calibration path and the Δt-reconstructibility property both rest on an unresolved dependency: Evidence.observed_at's intended meaning is not yet confirmed against DDR-002, and the record itself states the derivation may need 'a dedicated derived-at field added to Evidence' if DDR-002 intends the pinned-source-freshness reading instead. A ruling whose reconstructibility guarantee (§3, load-bearing for §6 calibration) forks on an unsettled upstream semantic is premature against that upstream — it commits to 'either way the capture instant is durably recoverable' before the branch that decides which mechanism is even known.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §6 'Evidence.observed_at's intended meaning must be confirmed against DDR-002 before adoption' + §3 Reconstructibility vs. DDR-002 v1.2.0 §4 (:Reasoning:Evidence) observed_at field (meaning unstated for the capture-instant reading)

findings.locus: Pre-Acceptance Condition 3 + Standing condition
findings.claim: The record asserts 'The ruling is sound as authored' while carrying three pre-acceptance conditions, two of which (Conditions 1 and 2) are DDR-002 amendments not yet authored on another ticket, plus a standing condition that its own load-bearing constants are empirically unvalidated at n=0 traffic. A DDR whose full mechanization depends on forthcoming amendments to its own ratified substrate is a candidate for ACCEPTED-WITH-CONDITIONS posture, not a ruling that 'stands as authored' — the reversibility question (does this shape deserve to stand now, before the DDR-002 carriers are settled) is not squarely posed by the record.

cited_authority.ref: author-decision-record SKILL: 'DDRs frequently land at ACCEPTED-WITH-CONDITIONS when prerequisite work must precede migration' + 'Conditions without tracking are wishes' — the record's conditions are tracked but the 'sound as authored' framing understates that acceptance is gated on unauthored upstream amendments

findings.locus: §2 Constant treatment (1.0 invariant) vs. ADR-001 §2.3 / DDR-002 §4
findings.claim: Attacked the 1.0-as-invariant ruling as a hidden fitness discrimination that would pull against the gateway's 'executes everything, authors nothing' posture — i.e., that fixing co-valid ground-truth options at equal 1.0 could be a place where the derivation silently makes a selection judgment. It held: the record explicitly separates epistemic confidence (is this fact true/current) from decision fitness (is this the better choice) and routes selection to the ASA's ReasoningProgress/RejectedAlternative/weight surfaces, consistent with ADR-001 §2.2's reasoning-capture locus and DDR-002 §4's inheritance-by-authority-class. The invariant reinforces the layering posture rather than pulling against it.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-001 v1.1.0 §2.2/§2.3 (locus of reasoning; gateway authors nothing) + DDR-002 v1.2.0 §4 (inheritance by authority class, not fitness)

findings.locus: §5 Composition boundary (branch (i) × #24 ceiling) vs. DDR-002 §4
findings.claim: Attacked the branch-(i)-inherits-node-authority-only boundary as a decision-bearing commitment that forecloses the routed ReasoningProgress rollup's access to per-target edge certainty — a position that could deserve its own record rather than a documented boundary. It held: the foreclosure is directional (upward only, under the #24 weakest-link ceiling max(Evidence.confidence)), the downward direction remains available below the ceiling via weight/aggregation, and the composition is routed to the already-designated solutioning-agent SDD per DDR-002 §4's rollup routing. The boundary is documented, not amended, and integrates with the ratified ceiling rather than committing a new undecided position.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 v1.2.0 §4 (#24 ceiling = max of supporting Evidence.confidence; rollup function routed to SDD) + §3 (OBSERVED_IN per-target edge confidence composed at rollup)

findings.locus: §4 Per-class dispositions — Environment DeploymentEnvironment (basis 2, base 0.9)
findings.claim: DDR-004 assigns Environment `DeploymentEnvironment` a non-aging flat base equal to the Environment base 0.9, and §2 fixes 0.9 as the contested Environment `base` tunable governed by §6 calibration. But §6 states `base` is 'fit to the baseline reliability of *fresh* Environment facts against outcomes' via re-observation of aging facts — a calibration procedure defined over the aging branch (ii). A non-aging `DeploymentEnvironment` produces no Δt and no re-observation staleness signal, so a re-tune of `base` driven by aging-fact calibration would silently move `DeploymentEnvironment`'s flat confidence, which the §6 calibration evidence never measured. The record couples `DeploymentEnvironment`'s flat base to a constant whose stated calibration basis does not include it.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §4 (DeploymentEnvironment basis-2 flat base = Environment base 0.9) in conflict with DDR-004 §6 ('base is fit to the baseline reliability of fresh Environment facts against outcomes' — aging-branch re-observation calibration)

findings.locus: §4 basis table / §5 composition boundary — Operational ObservedPattern basis 1 vs. edge certainty
findings.claim: §4 assigns Operational `ObservedPattern` basis 1 (inherit the node's own `confidence`), and §1 branch (i) names it 'lesson reliability.' §5 states the `OBSERVED_IN` per-target edge certainty is 'not inherited into Evidence.confidence' and composes downstream. DDR-002 §2.3/§3 fix `ObservedPattern` as carrying BOTH a node `confidence` (lesson reliability) AND an `OBSERVED_IN` edge `confidence` (per-target observation certainty). Basis 1 as authored — 'inherit the node's own confidence' — is exactly what §5 confirms; the two sections are consistent on inheritance, but §4's basis table entry 'as encoded in the node's confidence' under Staleness makes no statement about the edge-certainty surface DDR-002 requires exist, and the record nowhere asserts that a citation of an ObservedPattern reaches only the node surface at capture. Whether branch (i) reads node-only is asserted in §5 prose but the §4 totality table (the load-bearing per-class contract) does not encode the node-vs-edge scope, leaving the totality claim underspecified against the DDR-002 dual-surface definition.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §4 basis-1 table entry (ObservedPattern) against DDR-002 §2.3/§3 (ObservedPattern node confidence + OBSERVED_IN edge confidence, dual surface) and DDR-004 §5 (edge certainty not inherited)

findings.authority_locus: DDR-004 §5 composition boundary (Item 7 ruling) + DDR-002 §4 confidence-surface canon + DDR-002 §2.3/§3 (ObservedPattern node confidence = lesson reliability; OBSERVED_IN edge confidence = per-target certainty)

findings.locus: §6 Reconstructibility / Pre-Acceptance Condition 2 — Evidence.observed_at semantic
findings.claim: DDR-004 §6 asserts the preferred mechanism 'fixes that `Evidence.observed_at` *is* the capture instant (the Δt "to" point; the cited node's `observed_at` — the "from" point — recovered via the version pin).' DDR-002 §4 defines `(:Reasoning:Evidence)` with `observed_at` (T2) but does not state its meaning as the capture instant; §3 (this record) defines `Δt = capture_instant − cited_node.observed_at`, using the CITED NODE's `observed_at` as the 'from' point. The record simultaneously (a) uses `cited_node.observed_at` as the freshness-source timestamp and (b) proposes `Evidence.observed_at` means the capture instant — two distinct semantics for the same-named `observed_at` field across the Evidence node and the cited node, with the Evidence.observed_at meaning explicitly flagged as unconfirmed against DDR-002 (Pre-Acceptance Condition 2). The owning definition (DDR-002 §4) supplies no capture-instant meaning for Evidence.observed_at; the record asserts a concrete property while deferring its confirmation.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §6 + Pre-Acceptance Condition 2 ('Evidence.observed_at is the capture instant', flagged unconfirmed) against DDR-002 §4 (Evidence.observed_at (T2) — no capture-instant meaning supplied by the owning schema)

findings.locus: §4 basis-carrier claim — 'These are DDR-002 amendments (Pre-Acceptance Conditions)' + §2 last paragraph
findings.claim: §4 states the declared basis 'is carried in the schema — for core planes in DDR-002 §2.2–2.5, for Extension planes in the `PlaneDefinition` registration contract (§2.6)' as DDR-002 amendments. DDR-002 §2.6 `PlaneDefinition` is defined with `plane_id`, `plane_name`, `version`, `status`, `attaches_to`, `property_schema`, provenance — it carries no per-node-label derivation-basis declaration field. §2.2–§2.5 core-plane node schemas carry no derivation-basis annotation. The record asserts a concrete schema-carried basis declaration as if delegating to DDR-002's registration contract, but DDR-002 as it currently stands does not supply that field; the record correctly labels these as amendments the record entails, yet §4's totality proof ('a plane declaring a citable node with no basis fails registration') depends on a registration-contract capability DDR-002 does not yet carry, making the totality-by-construction claim contingent on an unratified amendment rather than the current substrate.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §4 ('declared basis is carried in the schema ... PlaneDefinition registration contract §2.6'; 'fails registration') against DDR-002 §2.6 PlaneDefinition schema (no derivation-basis field) and §2.2–§2.5 (no basis annotation)

findings.authority_locus: DDR template (ddr-template.md) — 'Pre-Acceptance / Migration Conditions' section + author-decision-record SKILL ('enumerate each condition as a discrete, buildable prerequisite with a tracking pointer'); DDR-004 deliberation record Authoring notes ('DDR-002 mechanization not authored here ... routed to RBT-54 Touch 3')

findings.locus: Reconciliation with the SDD-001 interim — 'Confirmed unchanged: ... the fail-closed CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE interim' vs §4 demotion
findings.claim: The Reconciliation section lists the fail-closed `CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE` interim under 'Confirmed unchanged' AND lists 'the per-class declared-basis totality (superseding the interim's single named CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE case)' under 'Fixed / extended by this record.' §4 demotes `CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE` from 'primary control' to 'defense-in-depth backstop ... unreachable for any well-formed plane.' SDD-001 §3.4.3 treats `CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE` as the reachable rejection for the confidence-less/timestamp-less Extension citation ('the known reachable case'). The record both confirms the interim unchanged and supersedes its primary-control role — the CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE case is simultaneously described as an unchanged confirmed behavior and as a superseded/demoted one; the reachability status also flips from SDD-001's 'known reachable case' to DDR-004's 'unreachable for any well-formed plane.'

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 Reconciliation section ('Confirmed unchanged: fail-closed CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE interim' vs 'Fixed/extended: per-class totality superseding the interim's single CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE case') and §4 ('unreachable for any well-formed plane') against SDD-001 §3.4.3 ('the known reachable case')

findings.authority_locus: DDR-004 §4 as ratified in the deliberation-record-ddr-004, Item 4 ruling: 'CONFIDENCE_UNDERIVABLE is demoted from primary control to defense-in-depth — unreachable for any well-formed plane'

findings.locus: §5 Composition boundary — branch (i) × #24 ceiling
findings.claim: Checked the §5 claim that branch (i) inherits node authority only and that per-target edge certainty composes downstream under the #24 ceiling, against DDR-002 §4's ceiling definition (ReasoningProgress.confidence ≤ max(SUPPORTED_BY Evidence.confidence)) and §3's confidence-surface split (node = lesson reliability; edge = per-target certainty). The claim holds: DDR-004's 'weakest-link, upward-foreclosed, downward-available-below-ceiling' reading is consistent with DDR-002 §4 (weight affects aggregation only below the ceiling) and routes composition to the solutioning-agent SDD, which SDD-001 §3.3.3 confirms returns the two surfaces uncomposed and routes the rollup. No contradiction surfaced across DDR-004 §5, DDR-002 §4/§3, and SDD-001 §3.3.3/§2.2.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §5 against DDR-002 §4 (#24 ceiling = max supporting Evidence.confidence; weight below ceiling) + §3 (node/edge confidence split) + SDD-001 §3.3.3 (track-record confidences returned uncomposed) / §2.2 (rollup routed)

findings.locus: §2 / §6 — 1.0 as invariant-ground vs contested base/τ
findings.claim: Attacked the §2 claim that the 1.0 class base is invariant-ground (not a §4.6 tunable) while base/τ are contested tunables, checking for internal inconsistency with §6's calibration scope and DDR-002 §4's 'inheritance by authority class' canon. The split holds: §6 explicitly scopes calibration to Environment base/τ only and states 1.0 is invariant, the form is fixed, and native bases inherit; DDR-002 §4 fixes inheritance by authority class and the ordering (authoritative Catalog above aging Environment) but not the literal 1.0 — so DDR-004's characterization of 1.0 as a determination-on-canonical-warrant rather than a value DDR-002 carries is consistent with the upstream. No self-contradiction between §2's invariant claim and §6's calibration exclusion.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §2 (1.0 invariant-ground, not §4.6 tunable) against DDR-004 §6 (calibration scoped to Environment base/τ; 1.0 invariant) and DDR-002 §4 (inheritance by authority class + ordering, not literal 1.0)
